description_long: This object is a classic hand grenade with a dark green, faceted metal body and a matte black safety lever and pin assembly on top. The body is segmented with angular ridges, giving it a robust, utilitarian appearance, and is speckled with lighter green and yellowish marks, suggesting wear or camouflage. A metallic gold band encircles the neck just below the safety mechanism, adding a distinct visual accent. The safety lever is elongated and curves downward, attached to a visible pull ring, which is used to arm the device. The top section is hexagonal and slightly raised, with a small, flat surface that may serve as a primer or fuse cap. The overall design is compact and functional, typical of military fragmentation grenades used in urban combat scenarios.
description: A dark green, faceted hand grenade with a black safety lever, pull ring, and a gold band around the neck. The surface is rugged and speckled, with a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the faceted green body, gold neck band, and the flat black top with minimal lever visibility.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended black safety lever and pull ring, with the gold band and faceted body clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the symmetrical, angular body and the black top, with the lever and ring partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the safety lever and ring, with the gold band and rugged body details.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is initiated by pulling the safety ring, which releases the lever and activates the internal fuse mechanism.
